paper.title: DisQ-HNet: A Disentangled Quantized Half-UNet for Interpretable Multimodal Image Synthesis Applications to Tau-PET Synthesis from T1 and FLAIR MRI
paper.abstract: Tau positron emission tomography (tau-PET) provides an in vivo marker of Alzheimer's disease pathology, but cost and limited availability motivate MRI-based alternatives. We introduce DisQ-HNet (DQH), a framework that synthesizes tau-PET from paired T1-weighted and FLAIR MRI while exposing how each modality contributes to the prediction. The method combines (i) a Partial Information Decomposition (PID)-guided, vector-quantized encoder that partitions latent information into redundant, unique, an

memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: DisQ-HNet reduces memory usage by 30% compared to UNet

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing pseudo-skip connections conditioned on structural edge cues can significantly improve model performance while reducing memory usage.
technical_details: Optimization techniques: AdamW, weight decay, and learning rate scheduling
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, but with limitations. DisQ-HNet can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices, but its performance may vary depending on the specific hardware configuration and available memory.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
